text.title: Service Object and Query Object Patterns
text.summary: Service Object with single #call method returning a domain entity, and Query Object with fluent builder accepting and returning a relation.

rule.statement: Service #call must return a domain entity or a Result monad — never nil, never void. Agents that return void prevent downstream composition from queue jobs and console commands.
rule.rationale: Void services require callers to re-fetch the entity after calling, creating implicit coupling. A returned entity enables callers to chain directly.

rule.statement: Wrap multi-write services in ActiveRecord::Base.transaction; never assume Eloquent-style auto-transaction.
rule.rationale: Creating a user then assigning roles in two separate AR calls is not atomic. A role assignment failure after a successful create leaves orphaned state without an explicit transaction.

example.code:
# app/services/users/create_service.rb (~50 lines) module Users class CreateService def initialize(params:, current_user: nil) @params = params @current_user = current_user end
def call validate! create_user send_welcome_email @user end
private
attr_reader :params, :current_user
def validate! raise ValidationError, "Email required" if params[:email].blank? raise ValidationError, "Email taken" if User.exists?(email: params[:email]) end
def create_user @user = User.create!( email: params[:email], name: params[:name], password: params[:password] ) end
def send_welcome_email Users::WelcomeEmailJob.perform_later(@user.id) end end end

example.note: Query objects accept and return relations, not arrays. Final .results (or .call) defers SQL execution.
